pattern: A size-k min-heap holds exactly the k largest values seen so far, so its smallest element is always the kth largest and each insertion costs only logarithmic time.
hints: You never need to sort the whole stream; only the top k values matter for the answer.
If you keep just the k biggest values, the smallest among them is the kth largest overall.
Maintain a min-heap capped at size k: push each new value, and if the heap exceeds k pop the smallest; the heap root is the answer.

edge_inputs.description: stream where later adds raise the running kth largest
duplicate scores count as separate elements when ranking
k equals one means the answer is always the maximum seen so far
empty initial list with k one builds up from the first add
negative values and a k larger than the initial list size

stress.description: moderate initial pool then a long sequence of adds with repeats and negatives
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    init_len = rng.randint(0, 50)
    nums = [rng.randint(-10000, 10000) for _ in range(init_len)]
    k = rng.randint(1, init_len + 1)
    ops = ['KthLargest']
    args = [[k, nums]]
    for _ in range(max(1, size)):
        ops.append('add'); args.append([rng.randint(-10000, 10000)])
    return [ops, args]
stress.note: k chosen within the valid 1..len+1 range; long add stream exercises heap maintenance
